nodes.context: Second-largest yakuza group in Japan, operates in upscale districts like Kabukichō and Ginza, involved in protection rackets.
Largest yakuza group in Japan, main rival of Sumiyoshi-kai.
Parent organization of Sumiyoshi-kai; its leaders became leaders of Sumiyoshi-kai.
Affiliated group of Sumiyoshi-kai; its head was gunned down in 2003.
Group whose head was found dead in 2007, raising tensions between Sumiyoshi-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi.

edges.context: Main rivals; involved in shootings and retributions in 2003 and 2007.
Sumiyoshi-kai is a confederation of smaller gangs, with Sumiyoshi-ikka as its parent organization; leaders of Sumiyoshi-ikka also lead Sumiyoshi-kai.
Nishida-gumi is an affiliated group of Sumiyoshi-kai.
Kokusui-kai is mentioned in context of tensions with Yamaguchi-gumi, implying affiliation with Sumiyoshi-kai.
